CBECS 2018 Building Energy Benchmarking Tool

Survey-weighted energy use intensity from the EIA 2018 Commercial Buildings Energy Consumption Survey public microdata. Each visitor's filters and results are independent — nothing here is shared between sessions.

FILTERS
manual
override →
Set State + County to auto-fill the zone, or leave both as "All" and pick a zone directly on the right.

(natural gas)
Compare to whole-building total above — gaps reflect fuel/end-use combinations not modeled by EIA (e.g. fuel oil and district heat end-use splits) and EIA disclosure-avoidance masking.
Source: U.S. EIA 2018 CBECS Public Use Microdata File & Variable and Response Codebook. County climate zone lookup derived from DOE Pacific Northwest National Laboratory's 2022 Guide to Determining Climate Zone by County (PNNL-33270, 2021 IECC/ASHRAE 169-2021 edition), translated to CBECS's five climate buckets per EIA's published mapping. EIA does not publish estimates from samples smaller than 20 buildings; this tool flags that threshold automatically. Results tabulated from the public microdata file will not exactly match EIA's published Detailed Tables, which use unmasked internal data.
